nation: Stoney First Nation - Morley Health Centre
one_time_description: COVID-19 emergency funding received from Indigenous Services Canada for public health measures, isolation centre and reopening costs
economic_notes: Entity operates as a health centre for Stoney First Nation. Related entities include Tribal Common, Big Horn Medical Services, Eden Valley Medical Services, Morley Common, Nakoda Contracting Ltd., Goodstoney First Nation, Chiniki First Nation, and Bearspaw First Nation. Defined contribution pension plan in place for employees.
notes: This is a program-level entity financial statement for the Morley Health Centre only, not the consolidated Stoney First Nation. The document notes that consolidated financial statements are prepared separately. Total government transfers calculated from deferred revenue note: Health Canada flexible $5,079,238 + ISC fixed $334,609 + Health Canada COVID $3,917,276 = $9,331,123. OSR = rental income $75,523 + other income $209,390 = $284,913. Net financial assets of $551,406 represents the operating fund balance; tangible capital assets of $2,559,940 represent the capital fund. Deferred revenue of $4,485,238 is significant. Advances from other First Nation entities of $519,320 received during year. Administration fees of $277,000 paid to Tribal Common for shared services. Rent paid to related First Nation entities totalling $251,375.
